title: sida aprica var. aprica
intro: Sida aprica var. aprica, also known as the annual sida, is a flowering plant species in the family Malvaceae. It is a shrub-like plant with medicinal properties, particularly in its leaves and roots, which contain flavonoids and saponins, exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been traditionally used for its health benefits, including treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as for its antiseptic and antifungal properties. Herbal preparations of Sida aprica var. aprica include infusions and decoctions made from the leaves and roots, which can be used to treat various health issues.
